milestone_brief: Implement roadmap epic realtime-variety-render-e4 -- SPLIT SCOPE: CAND-4 (background-thread worker) ONLY. CAND-3 (coarse-preview LOD) is deferred to a follow-up milestone (e4b) per the user scope decision and roadmap section 6.3. Move surface.generate() off the Qt GUI thread onto a QThread/QRunnable worker. Worker dispatch replaces the direct surface.generate() call in app.py; the _computing re-entrancy guard semantics change from blocking to worker-in-flight; the QApplication.processEvents() workaround is removed; _apply_domain_and_render receives the worker result via a Qt.QueuedConnection signal. VTK GitLab #18782 mitigation (mandatory, from the e3 spike report): the worker retains an explicit Python ref to the output mesh (self._result = mesh) before emitting; the main-thread slot retains the ref immediately on receipt. The e1 queue-latest semantics (_computing guard + _pending_render) must be extended to cover the worker-in-flight state for the rapid cancel-and-resubmit / supersede path. All VTK GL operations (add_mesh, render, plotter.*) stay on the GUI thread -- never in the worker. NO coarse-LOD, NO coarse_n floor table, NO AI-15 Preview badge in this milestone (those are CAND-3 / e4b). Hard constraints: AI-1 (PySide6+PyVista+pyvistaqt only; no PySide6 pin change -- pyvistaqt 0.11.4 already has the #793 fix), AI-2 (Qt-free tests -- the worker lifecycle / cancel-resubmit has a known coverage gap, document it in CONTEXT.md section 9), AI-3 (pv.OFF_SCREEN headless verification, never MainWindow under offscreen), AI-9 (re-entrancy re-analysis for the worker dispatch path). Predecessor e3 spike (.claude/notes/roadmaps/realtime-variety-render/spike-cand4-thread-safety.md) verdict: e4 cleared to proceed. The macOS on-device verification checklist (spike section 7) is a documented pre-ship manual gate -- it cannot run on this Windows dev machine; implementation proceeds on Windows per the spike guidance.
research_synthesis: Both briefs converge: QThreadPool+QRunnable worker (e3-spike pattern), WorkerSignals(QObject) carrier emitting a job-id-tagged payload via Qt.QueuedConnection. The whole _render_current try-body (warnings.catch_warnings, perf_counter timing, surface.generate, ValueError/Exception capture) moves into worker.run(); VTK #18782 mitigation: worker self._result=mesh before emit, main-thread slot retains mesh first line. Briefs diverged only on the _computing guard: synthesis adopts agent-a's lower-risk option -- keep the e1 _computing bool + _pending_render queue-latest coalescing (one worker in flight, latest request queued) AND add a monotonic _generation counter so the result slot defensively discards any stale signal. processEvents() removed; _on_domain_changed stays synchronous; closeEvent drains QThreadPool.waitForDone(). Extract a Qt-free is_stale_result() free function for unit testing; document the worker-lifecycle test gap (AI-2) in CONTEXT.md.
